input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

data.content:
`.testsettings` files are **legacy** and no longer supported by MSTest v3+. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's how your three settings map:

| `.testsettings` | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property | `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| Code coverage data collector | `<DataCollector>` in `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTypeSpecific/AssemblyResolution deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces Properties/TestTimeout (value in milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces AgentRule code-coverage DataCollector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution / test deployment config
2. Test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file, which is the modern format supported by MSTest v3+. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and no longer supported by newer MSTest versions.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that maps each setting appropriately.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces TestTypeSpecific/AssemblyResolution deployment behaviour -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
    <!-- Replaces Properties/TestTimeout (value in milliseconds) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces AgentRule code-coverage DataCollector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Created `local.runsettings` with all three settings migrated. To use it:

- **CLI:** `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- **csproj (auto-use):** add `<RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to a `<PropertyGroup>`
- **Visual Studio:** *Test → Configure Run Settings → Select Solution Wide runsettings File*

Once confirmed working, you can delete the old `local.testsettings` file.
